div1.entryFree:
Catch, subst., 1) the act of catching, or the thing caught: no doubt but he hath got a quiet c. Shr. II, 333. Hector shall have a great c., if he knock out either of your brains, Troil. II, 1, 110.
2) a song sung in succession: Tp. III, 2, 126. Tp. III, 2, 126 Tw. II, 3, 18. Tw. II, 3, 18 Tw. II, 3, 18 Tw. II, 3, 18*
